mnemonics: TCP Segments Are Like Packed Suitcases: They carry a header (tag) and payload (clothes) to a specific destination port.
keyPoints: A segment is the Protocol Data Unit (PDU) used at the Transport Layer (Layer 4).
It is created when data from the application layer is encapsulated with a Transport Layer header (TCP or UDP).
The header contains key information like source/destination port numbers, sequence numbers, and checksums for reliable data transfer.
commonMistakes: Confusing segments (Transport Layer) with packets (Network Layer) or frames (Data Link Layer).
Assuming all segments are TCP; UDP also creates segments, but they are often called datagrams.
